title: The Conditionnel Présent
description: A2 introduction to the present conditional in French: formation from the futur simple stem plus imparfait endings, polite requests, wishes, advice, and a first look at si-clauses. Explained in English.

cards.front: je parlerais / tu parlerais / il parlerait / nous parlerions / vous parleriez / ils parleraient
cards.notes:
The present conditional = **futur simple stem** + **imparfait endings**. For regular -er verbs the futur stem is the infinitive itself: *parler-*. Then add: *-ais, -ais, -ait, -ions, -iez, -aient*.

| Person | Ending |
|---|---|
| je | **-ais** |
| tu | **-ais** |
| il/elle | **-ait** |
| nous | **-ions** |
| vous | **-iez** |
| ils/elles | **-aient** |

cards.front: être → ser- / avoir → aur- / aller → ir- / faire → fer- / vouloir → voudr- / pouvoir → pourr- / devoir → devr-
cards.notes:
The conditional uses the **same irregular stems as the futur simple** — only the endings change. Memorise the stem, then add the imparfait ending.

| Verb | Irregular stem | je-form |
|---|---|---|
| être | **ser-** | je **serais** |
| avoir | **aur-** | j'**aurais** |
| aller | **ir-** | j'**irais** |
| faire | **fer-** | je **ferais** |
| vouloir | **voudr-** | je **voudrais** |
| pouvoir | **pourr-** | je **pourrais** |
| devoir | **devr-** | je **devrais** |
cards.hint: Same irregular stems as the futur simple — learn them once, use them twice.

cards.front: je voudrais / j'aimerais / pourriez-vous... ? / est-ce que vous auriez... ?
cards.notes:
The conditional is the default politeness tense in French.

| Expression | Literal meaning | Use |
|---|---|---|
| **je voudrais** | I would like | ordering, requesting |
| **j'aimerais** | I would like / love | softer wish |
| **pourriez-vous** | could you | polite request to someone |
| **est-ce que vous auriez** | would you have | very polite inquiry |

Compare: *Je **veux** un cafe* (blunt) vs *Je **voudrais** un cafe* (polite). A1 link: you may already know *je voudrais* — it is the conditional of *vouloir*.

cards.back: you should (advice with devoir in the conditional)
cards.notes: *Tu **devrais** faire du sport* = You should do some exercise. The conditional of *devoir* softens a command into a recommendation. Contrast with the present tense: *tu **dois*** = you must (obligation).

cards.front: Si j'avais le temps, je voyagerais.
cards.back: If I had the time, I would travel. (si + imparfait → conditionnel)
cards.notes:
A **conditional sentence** links a hypothetical condition (the si-clause) to its imagined result:

> **si + imparfait** → **conditionnel**

*Si j'**avais** de l'argent, j'**acheterais** une voiture.* (If I **had** money, I **would buy** a car.)

Never use the conditionnel directly after *si*: *si j'aurais* is wrong in standard French.
cards.hint: After si (if), use the imparfait — NOT the conditional.

cards.back: I would like (polite conditional of vouloir)
cards.notes: *Je **voudrais** un cafe, s'il vous plait.* (I would like a coffee, please.) This is A1-level vocabulary that is actually A2 grammar: the conditional of *vouloir*.
Stem: *ser-* + *-ais* → *je **serais***. Example: *Je **serais** heureux de vous aider.* (I would be happy to help you.)

Stem: *aur-* + *-ais* → *j'**aurais***. Example: *J'**aurais** besoin d'aide.* (I would need some help.) Often appears in polite expressions: *Est-ce que vous **auriez** un stylo ?* (Would you have a pen?)

cards.back: I would do / make (conditional of faire)
cards.notes: Stem: *fer-* + *-ais* → *je **ferais***. Example: *Qu'est-ce que tu **ferais** a ma place ?* (What would you do in my position?)

cards.back: I could / could you (conditional of pouvoir)
cards.notes: Stem: *pourr-* + ending. *Je **pourrais** venir demain.* (I could come tomorrow.) *Pourriez-vous repeter, s'il vous plait ?* (Could you repeat that, please?) — a key politeness phrase.

cards.back: you should leave (conditional of devoir = advice)
cards.notes: *Devoir* in the conditional gives soft advice: *Tu **devrais** te reposer.* (You should rest.) Compare: *tu **dois** partir* (you must leave — stronger obligation).
Stem: *ir-* + *-ais* → *j'**irais***. Example: *Si j'etais libre, j'**irais** en France.* (If I were free, I would go to France.)

steps.title: Formation: futur stem + imparfait endings
steps.body:
# The Conditionnel Présent

The **present conditional** expresses what *would* happen. It is formed from two pieces you already know:

> **futur simple stem** + **imparfait endings**

## The endings

The endings are identical to those of the imparfait:

| Person | Ending | Example (parler) |
|---|---|---|
| je | **-ais** | je parler**ais** |
| tu | **-ais** | tu parler**ais** |
| il / elle | **-ait** | il parler**ait** |
| nous | **-ions** | nous parler**ions** |
| vous | **-iez** | vous parler**iez** |
| ils / elles | **-aient** | ils parler**aient** |

## The stem

For **regular** verbs the stem is the infinitive (same as the futur):
- *parler → parler-* → *je parlerais*
- *finir → finir-* → *je finirais*

For **irregular** verbs the stem changes — but it is the **same** stem as the futur simple:

| Verb | Stem | je-form |
|---|---|---|
| être | **ser-** | je **serais** |
| avoir | **aur-** | j'**aurais** |
| aller | **ir-** | j'**irais** |
| faire | **fer-** | je **ferais** |
| vouloir | **voudr-** | je **voudrais** |
| pouvoir | **pourr-** | je **pourrais** |
| devoir | **devr-** | je **devrais** |

steps.title: Uses: politeness, wishes, advice
steps.body:
## When to use the conditionnel

### 1. Polite requests

The conditional is the standard politeness register in French. Using the present tense can sound abrupt:

| Blunt (present) | Polite (conditional) | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| Je veux un cafe. | **Je voudrais** un cafe. | I would like a coffee. |
| Tu peux m'aider ? | **Pourriez-vous** m'aider ? | Could you help me? |
| Tu as un stylo ? | **Est-ce que vous auriez** un stylo ? | Would you have a pen? |

> **A1 link:** *je voudrais* (I would like) is already polite conditional — you may have been using it without realising it is a grammatical tense!

### 2. Wishes

*J'aimerais voyager en Asie.* (I would love to travel in Asia.)

### 3. Advice with devoir

*tu **devrais*** = you should (soft recommendation)

*Tu devrais manger plus de legumes.* (You should eat more vegetables.)

Contrast: *tu **dois*** = you must (strong obligation).

steps.title: Hypothetical si-clauses: si + imparfait → conditionnel
steps.body:
## Conditional sentences (si-clauses)

A classic structure links an **imaginary condition** to its **result**:

> **si + imparfait** → **conditionnel present**

| Si-clause (imparfait) | Main clause (conditionnel) | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| Si j'**avais** le temps | je **voyagerais**. | If I had the time, I would travel. |
| Si elle **etait** libre | elle **viendrait**. | If she were free, she would come. |
| Si nous **avions** de l'argent | nous **acheterions** une maison. | If we had money, we would buy a house. |

## Important rule

Never place the conditional directly after *si*:

- **Wrong:** *Si j'aurais le temps...* (common English-speaker error)
- **Right:** *Si j'**avais** le temps...*

Think of it as: *si* gets the imparfait, the other clause gets the conditionnel.

steps.title: Match the verb to its conditional stem

exercise.prompt: Match each verb to its conditional/futur stem.

steps.title: Polite request: I would like a coffee

exercise.prompt: Translate politely: *I would like a coffee, please.* (Use je voudrais.)
accept: Je voudrais un cafe, s'il vous plait.
je voudrais un cafe, s'il vous plait
Je voudrais un cafe s'il vous plait.
Je voudrais un café, s'il vous plaît.
je voudrais un café s'il vous plaît
exercise.hint: vouloir → voudr- + -ais = voudrais. Do not write je veux (that is blunt).
distractors: Je veux un cafe, s'il vous plait.
Je voudrai un cafe, s'il vous plait.
Je voudrais un cafe, si vous plait.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the conditional of *devoir* (tu) to give soft advice.
exercise.sentence: Tu ___ te reposer, tu as l'air fatigue.

steps.title: Build: If I had time, I would travel

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words to say: *If I had the time, I would travel.*
A customer is ordering in a restaurant. Which sentence is the polite choice?

images.label: Je voudrais un steak, s'il vous plait.

exercise.hint: The conditional *voudrais* is polite; the present *veux* is blunt; *voudrai* (futur) is incorrect here.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *Could you repeat that, please?* (Use pourriez-vous.)
exercise.hint: pouvoir → pourr- + -iez (vous form) = pourriez. Then the infinitive: repeter.

steps.title: Wish: I would go to France

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I would go to France if I had the money.* (Start with the wish clause.)
accept: J'irais en France si j'avais l'argent.
j'irais en France si j'avais l'argent
J'irais en France si j'avais de l'argent.
j'irais en France si j'avais de l'argent
exercise.hint: aller → ir- + -ais = irais. After si: imparfait of avoir (avais).
distractors: J'irai en France si j'avais l'argent.
J'irais en France si j'aurais l'argent.
Je vais en France si j'avais l'argent.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the conditional of *faire* (je) to complete this si-clause sentence.
exercise.sentence: Si j'etais en vacances, je ___ du sport tous les jours.

steps.title: Match the conditional form to its English meaning

exercise.prompt: Match each French conditional expression to its English meaning.

steps.title: Build: I would be happy to help

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *I would be happy to help you.*
Translate: *You (tu) should sleep more.* (Use devoir in the conditional for soft advice.)
exercise.hint: devoir → devr- + -ais (tu) = devrais. Not tu dois (too strong).
